content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission



May 6, 1994


Dwight M. Beattie
Registrar of Voters
Santa Clara County
1553 Berger Drive
P.O. Box 1147
San Jose, CA 95108

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-94-156

Dear Mr. Beattie:

This is to confirm advice provided to you on May 4, 1994
regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act.1

Measure B was a school bond measure for the Mountain View-Los
Altos Union High School District, on the April 12, 1994 ballot.
However, because of problems in the mailing of absentee ballots,
the results of that election were annulled by the Superior Court.
The Court also ordered the County to run the election again,
concurrent with the June 7, 1994 Primary Election. What was
Measure B on the April ballot will be Measure I in the June
election. Committees which are primarily formed to support or
oppose Measures B and I should file according to the following
schedule:


1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807• (916) 322-5660

Beattie, Dwight
Page 2
No. I-94-156


Type of Statement          Period Covered          Filing Deadline

1st Pre-Election           Jan. 1- Feb. 26        March 3

2nd Pre-Election           Feb. 27 - Mar. 26      March 31

May Pre-Election           Mar. 27 - May 21       May 26

Semi-Annual                May 22 - June 30       July 31


     If any committees which are primarily formed to support or
oppose Measure I, become qualified as a committee after April 12
but before May 22, they should file one pre-election statement
covering from January 1 through May 21, due May 26.

     If you have any additional questions, please contact me at
(916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Wayne Imberi
                                        Political Reform Consultant

sections.question_synthetic: What are the campaign disclosure filing requirements and schedules for committees supporting or opposing Measure B (later Measure I) in Santa Clara County, given that the April 12, 1994 election was annulled and rescheduled for June 7, 1994?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Committees primarily formed to support or oppose Measure I must file campaign disclosure statements according to a specific schedule with four filing deadlines: March 3 (1st Pre-Election), March 31 (2nd Pre-Election), May 26 (May Pre-Election), and July 31 (Semi-Annual). Committees that qualify after April 12 but before May 22 should file one consolidated pre-election statement by May 26.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is confirmation of telephone advice provided May 4, 1994. No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections present; question and conclusion extracted from body text. Contains statutory references and detailed filing schedule table. Confidence reduced slightly due to lack of formal section headers.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the campaign disclosure filing requirements and schedules for committees supporting or opposing Measure B (later Measure I) in Santa Clara County, given that the April 12, 1994 election was annulled and rescheduled for June 7, 1994?

CONCLUSION: Committees primarily formed to support or oppose Measure I must file campaign disclosure statements according to a specific schedule with four filing deadlines: March 3 (1st Pre-Election), March 31 (2nd Pre-Election), May 26 (May Pre-Election), and July 31 (Semi-Annual). Committees that qualify after April 12 but before May 22 should file one consolidated pre-election statement by May 26.

However, because of problems in the mailing of absentee ballots, the results of that election were annulled by the Superior Court. The Court also ordered the County to run the election again, concurrent with the June 7, 1994 Primary Election. What was Measure B on the April ballot will be Measure I in the June election. Committees which are primarily formed to support or oppose Measures B and I should file according to the following schedule: 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming campaign disclosure filing requirements and deadlines for committees supporting or opposing Measure I (formerly Measure B) in a Santa Clara County school bond election that was rescheduled from April 12 to June 7, 1994 due to absentee ballot mailing problems.
